source: The Forever Winter shipping game files (build 24045295) — FWAIPawnDefinition enemy defs + BP_AI health components + weapon defs, decoded with CUE4Parse
note: Datamined straight from each unit's AI pawn definition: threat class, kill XP, the stagger/stun threshold, the grab (sync-kill) HP threshold & range, melee & dash damage, mounted weapons and (for mechs) layered armour zones. HP for the smaller units is read from the unit's blueprint health component. The 10 bosses keep hand-written tactics; every other unit is a datamined one-liner.

codex.note: Every boss drops a container on defeat; drill it (~60 s) for a guaranteed Codex — 62,500 XP and ~32,800 cr, used in max-rank Innards upgrades, the Cognition Cradle, and some quests.

staggerNote: “Stagger” = burst this much damage into it within the window and it's stunned; only player damage counts. This is why a single railgun shot can freeze a target a whole magazine can't.

categories.name: Infantry
categories.blurb: Humanoid personnel — the troops, snipers, cyborgs and elites that make up most of every squad.

categories.name: Drones
categories.blurb: Small hovering and walking robots — spotters, attackers and support units. Most can't be stunned.

categories.name: Turrets
categories.blurb: Fixed and deployable guns. They can't chase you — break line of sight and they're beaten.

categories.name: Aircraft
categories.blurb: Flying gunships. Heavy firepower, effectively unstaggerable — fight them from hard cover.

categories.name: Vehicles
categories.blurb: Tanks and armoured vehicles. Cannon + coaxial MG, heavily armoured, worth big XP.

categories.name: Mechs & Giants
categories.blurb: The walking war-machines and giant automatons — the apex threats, most with layered armour and instakill grabs.

units.name: Egghead
units.desc: A lightly-armed Europan technician (~940 HP) — low threat, and often an objective or escort target rather than a fight.

units.name: Scrambler
units.desc: The unit that jams your radar and HUD when it's nearby (~1,500 HP). Kill it first to get your detection back.

units.name: Pursuer
units.desc: A light hunter that breaks off to chase YOU specifically. Fragile (~940 HP) but stagger-resistant, so it won't flinch when hit.

units.name: Signaler
units.desc: Calls in reinforcements — silence it fast or the encounter snowballs. Fragile (~940 HP).

units.name: Sniper
units.desc: A soldier that hangs back and reaches out: low HP, but the shot that finds you comes from off-screen. Its vision reaches twice as far as a grunt's — stay out of the arc.

senses.notes: Sees twice as far as a soldier in a near-180° arc — stay out of sightlines. (The 'Deadly Sniper' variant shares this exact profile.)

units.name: Cultist
units.desc: Cult of the Leech fanatics (~1,875 HP) — they come as riflemen, grenadiers and heavy-weapons variants, erratic and aggressive.

units.name: Soldier
units.desc: The baseline trooper — a rifle, ~1,250 HP and no special tricks. Euruskan soldiers are the exception: far tougher (~4,600 HP) and flagged high-threat.
units.variants: Europa / Eurasia / Euruska / Scav; sleeveless, VIP-guard & rider skins

senses.notes: The baseline grunt. In-front and within 40 m is the danger zone; 14 m is near-guaranteed.

units.name: Water Thief
units.desc: Rival scavengers (~2,800 HP) raiding the same maps you are — skirmishers, looters and berzerkers, all after the same water you need.

units.name: Cyborg
units.desc: A Eurasian melee drone-soldier (~2,200 HP) that rushes in to claw at you — it has headshot resistance, so put rounds in the body, not the head.

units.name: EOD
units.desc: Europa's armoured elite — tanky (~4,600 HP), grabs at ≤550 HP, and the named ones (Jack, Pyro, Hunter-Killer) resist stagger. Pyro brings fire.

units.name: Heavy
units.desc: A high-threat gunner with far more HP than a rifleman (~4,600) and an MG that pins you down. Can grab you if you drop below ~550 HP.

units.name: Brawler
units.desc: The toughest infantry in the game (~9,250 HP) — a helmeted melee bruiser that closes and grabs you at ≤400 HP.

units.name: Grabber
units.blurb: The thing that runs you down and pulls you in half.
units.desc: A fast, bipedal-mechanical terror that stalks the map, then closes with leaps and a mauling melee. Its chip damage is trivial — the danger is the grab: get inside arm's reach and it plays a Smash sync-kill that ends the raid on the spot, even at near-full health. Its AI is internally the “Stalker” (the art and codex call it the Grabber), and Toothy borrows its ability set.
units.location: Most maps; hunts day and night
weaknesses: Can't open doors or fit tight gaps — break line of sight behind a gate and it's stuck
Its own melee only does 600–900; the grab is the only thing that actually kills you
Can be stunned — a burst of 20,000 in 3 s (a railgun hit) staggers it

senses.notes: Exceptional hearing (40 m) and a tall vertical cone; doubles its ESP radius. Built to hunt.

units.name: Heal Drone
units.desc: A support drone that heals its owners (0 kill XP). No threat to you directly, but it keeps everything else alive — pop it first.

units.name: Quad Drone
units.desc: A four-legged walker-drone with an enormous HP pool but little kill value — usually not worth focusing down.

units.name: Attack Drone
units.desc: A hovering recon/attack drone (~2,500 HP) fielded by every faction. Can't be staggered, and it plays an instakill grab if you fall below ~450 HP.

senses.notes: Narrow 90° cone but wide vertical arc — it looks down well.

units.name: Walking Turret
units.desc: A turret that walks into position and pops up to fire (~9,000 HP) — it can reposition, unlike a fixed emplacement.

units.name: Turret
units.desc: A static emplaced gun (~7,000 HP). It can't move — break its line of sight and it's neutralised. The 20mm version is flagged high-threat.

senses.notes: The ordinary mounted turret — not the Scav 360° one. A wide 195° arc to 40 m at a soldier's lock speed, plus a 4.5× ESP bubble that sees through walls up close. Come at it from behind, or from well past 40 m.

units.name: Chopper
units.desc: A Eurasian attack helicopter (~66,750 HP) with a 300-damage minigun. Effectively unstaggerable; the Hunter-Killer gunship variant hits harder. Fight it from hard cover.

senses.notes: Long range but very slow to lock (12–18 s) — you can cross gaps between its sweeps.

units.name: Opal
units.blurb: The one that circles overhead and strafes you.
units.desc: An airborne Eurasian gunship-mech that flies scripted maneuver splines — circle, figure-8, zig-zag, dog-leg, leap-over — while strafing with dual rapid-fire gun-hands (16.7 per shot, full-auto). Its “Glide of Doom” dash deals 800 over an 8–20 m lunge. Very nearly impossible to stagger — threshold 99,999.
weaknesses: You will not stun it — 99,999 stagger is effectively immune
Flies predictable maneuver splines — lead its path
Grab (Shoot sync-kill) only at ≤700 HP

senses.notes: A named Eurasia elite in a near-180° arc. Only 40 m range, but she locks almost as fast at the edge as up close (1.5 s far vs a grunt's 2.75) — there's no safe lingering distance. Killing her spawns an Opal Hunter-Killer.

units.name: Merkava Tank
units.desc: Europa's main battle tank — cannon + coaxial MG, heavily armoured. A quest variant serves as a body-boss objective.

senses.notes: Wide arc, long range, slow build.

units.name: T-90 Tank
units.desc: A Euruskan main battle tank: a main cannon plus a coaxial MG, heavily armoured and unstaggerable. Worth 1,850 XP, but you'll need anti-tank firepower to earn it.

senses.notes: Wide arc, long range, slow build.

units.name: Tripod
units.desc: A small three-legged walker-drone — a big HP pool but low kill value, usually safe to skip.

units.name: Exo
units.desc: A Europan bipedal battle-mech behind ~105,900 of layered armour. Twin stubby guns and a 167-damage arm cannon, plus a grab at ≤900 HP. The named Red Baron, Jolly Rodger and Downtown versions are all this chassis.

senses.notes: Enormous 400 m vision, but slow to build detection (4–9 s) — you have a moment before it commits.

units.name: Stilt Walker
units.desc: A towering Europan walker on stilt legs — ~130,900 of layered armour, a minigun and four 40mm-HE missile launchers (150 damage each) plus a stomp. Grabs at ≤900 HP. Serious area denial.

senses.notes: Enormous 400 m vision, but slow to build detection (4–9 s) — you have a moment before it commits.

units.name: Blind Mother
units.blurb: Mother Courage with her eyes gone — she hunts by sound alone.
units.desc: The same devastating kit as Mother Courage — five-hit AOE, an at-any-health Rip/Chomp grab — but she is blind. No vision sensor at all, just a 30 m instant-hearing cylinder. She literally cannot see you.
weaknesses: Blind — crouch-moving emits no noise event, so a silent approach is invisible to her
Sprinting or shooting nearby is what brings the grab

senses.notes: Runs on hearing — a 30 m listen radius with an instant lock. Move slow and quiet; do not sprint near her. (She does carry a long, flat vision cone in the files, but it builds so slowly it never matters — the fight is entirely about noise.)

units.name: MeatMan
units.blurb: Closes two hundred metres in a single lunge.
units.desc: A Euruskan melee brute running the Orga brain. Eight-hit combo (3,000 each) and a Stab sync-kill — but its signature is the dash: 800 damage over a 0.4–200 m range on a 6 s cooldown. It can cross almost the entire arena in one charge.
weaknesses: The 200 m dash is on a 6 s cooldown and commits — sidestep the tell
Grab only at ≤700 HP
Stagger window: 20,000 in 4 s

units.name: Medium Mech
units.blurb: The standard walking tank — and the chassis Rat King and Toothy are built on.
units.desc: The workhorse bipedal war-mech, fielded by every faction. It grinds you down with a long rifle, dual quad-guns and two miniguns, plus an 8,000-damage ground-pound up close. Its armour is split into zones totalling roughly 451,000 HP.
units.weakpoint: Aim the **exposed lower torso** — the waist/belly joint where the upper body pivots over the hips (the datamined `armor_vulnerable` zone, mesh `SM_Spine_jnt__EXO_TorsoPivotBottom`). It takes **full** damage, while the heavy chest & leg plating (200,000 HP) soaks 90%. The cockpit glass isn't it — that panel is just an info display.
weaknesses: Weak spot: the exposed lower-torso / waist joint takes full damage; the plating soaks up to 90%
Its guns are destructible hardpoints — blow off the shoulder quad-guns and arm cannon to defang it
Stagger window: 20,000 in 4 s
Small arms barely scratch the plating — hit the weak spot, or bring anti-tank / railgun class

senses.notes: Enormous 400 m vision, but slow to build detection (4–9 s) — you have a moment before it commits.

units.name: Mother Courage
units.blurb: She walks you down and pulls you apart — at any health.
units.desc: A towering, female-form automaton that stalks the wastes with immobile cyborgs riveted to her frame. Her melee is a five-hit AOE (8k/6k/6k/8k/6k). The nightmare is her grab: a Rip or Chomp sync-kill that triggers regardless of your health — no health pool saves you, only distance.
weaknesses: Slow — you can simply out-walk her
Stagger window: 30,000 in 4 s
Has a scripted anti-boss self-destruct sequence

units.name: Orgamech
units.blurb: A fleshy melee mech that dashes in and stabs.
units.desc: A humanoid organic-mech. An eight-hit melee combo (3,000 each), an 800-damage dash gap-closer (0.4–10 m, 10 s cooldown), and a Stab sync-kill. The easiest of the big bosses to stagger, at 20,000.
units.location: Euruskan maps; obtainable in the Tunnels
weaknesses: Grab only at ≤700 HP — stay above it
Lowest stagger threshold of the melee bosses — 20,000
Dash telegraphs on a 10 s cooldown

units.name: Rat King
units.blurb: A Medium Mech the Scavengers stole and bolted junk onto.
units.desc: Mechanically a Medium Mech — same ~451,000 HP layered armour and mech guns (Rat King weapon variants) — but Scav-piloted and noticeably easier to stun (12,000 vs the standard 20,000). It drops its loot container on death, and its parts are a fetch-quest objective.
units.weakpoint: Same Medium Mech chassis, so the same weak spot — the exposed lower-torso / waist joint takes full damage while the plating soaks up to 90%.
weaknesses: Easiest mech to stagger — only 12,000 burst in the window
Drops its container (and the codex) on death

senses.notes: Enormous 400 m vision, but slow to build detection (4–9 s) — you have a moment before it commits.

units.name: Shield Officer
units.blurb: Hides, teleports, and shield-stabs you when you least expect it.
units.desc: A shield-bearing Euruskan officer that teleports and relocates to hiding spots mid-fight (its behaviour tree literally has “teleport-to” and “find-hiding-location” tasks). Shares the Orga melee kit — eight-hit combo, 800 dash — plus a Shield-Stab sync-kill. The tankiest stagger threshold of the humanoids at 40,000.
weaknesses: Grab only at ≤700 HP
Repositions constantly — expect it to vanish and reappear
High stagger threshold — 40,000 needs a heavy single hit

senses.notes: Soldier-range vision in a wide arc.

units.name: Toothy
units.blurb: The one with the digestive sacks. It eats what it grabs.
units.desc: A blocky, ground-shaking Euruskan mech that patrols the flat deserts of Ashen Mesa. A cannon railgun and a left-shoulder machine gun, plus an 8,000-damage Stomp AOE. It grabs troops and scavengers into black digestive sacks (a Bag or Smash sync-kill) and expels the skeletons.
units.weakpoint: Built on the same Exo mech chassis, so the vulnerable zone is the exposed lower torso (the waist/pivot joint under the chest) — it takes full damage while the plating soaks up to 90%.
weaknesses: Grab only triggers at ≤700 HP — stay healthy and it can't bag you
Stagger window: 30,000 in 4 s
Layered armour — hit the vulnerable zone

senses.notes: Enormous 400 m vision, but slow to build detection (4–9 s) — you have a moment before it commits.
